questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A thin superconducting (zero resistance) ring is held above a vertical long solenoid, as shown in the figure. The axis of symmetry of the ring is same to that of the solenoid. The cylindrically symmetric magnetic field around the ring can be described approximately in terms of the vertical and radial component of the magnetic field vector as $B_z=B_d(1-\alpha z)$ and $B_r=B_0 \beta r$, where $B_0, \alpha$ and $\beta$ are positive constants, and $z \& r$ are vertical and radial position coordinates, respectively. Initially plane of the ring is horizontal, has no current flowing in it. When released, it starts to move downwards with its axis still vertical. Initial coordinates of the centre of the ring ' O ' is $\mathrm{z}=0$ and $\mathrm{r}=0$. In the given diagram point $O$ is on the axis and slightly above the solenoid having vertical and radial position coordinates as ( 0,0 ). Ring has mass m , radius $\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{e}}$ and self inductance L . Assume the acceleration due to gravity as g.
Question: Find the magnitude of current in the ring at a vertical position z .
questions.solution:
Total magnetic flux at any position $\phi=\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{z}} \pi \mathrm{r}_0^2-\mathrm{LI}$
Since, $R=0$, so $\phi=B_0(1-\alpha z) \pi r_0^2-L I=$ constant
From initial condition $(z=0, I=0)$, the value of constant is
$$
\phi=\mathrm{B}_0 \pi \mathrm{r}_0^2
$$
Using the above equation the current in the ring
$$
\mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{~L}} \mathrm{~B}_0 \alpha \pi \mathrm{r}_0^2 \mathrm{z}
$$
The lorentz force acting on the ring (which can only be vertical, because of the symmetry of the assembly) can be expressed as
$$
\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{z}}=-\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{z}) 2 \pi \mathrm{r}_0=-\frac{2 \mathrm{~B}_0^2 \alpha \beta \pi^2 \mathrm{r}_0^4 \mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{~L}}=-\mathrm{kz}
$$
Equation of motion of the ring is $\mathrm{ma}_{\mathrm{z}}=\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{z}}-\mathrm{mg}=-\mathrm{kz}-\mathrm{mg}$
Equilibrium position $\mathrm{z}_0=-\mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{k}$
$$
\omega_0=\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Paragraph: A thin superconducting (zero resistance) ring is held above a vertical long solenoid, as shown in the figure. The axis of symmetry of the ring is same to that of the solenoid. Ring has mass m , radius $\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{e}}$ and self inductance L . Assume the acceleration due to gravity as g
Question: Find the time period of SHM (for small displacement along z-axis) of the ring.
options:
ring will not perform SHM
Total magnetic flux at any position $\phi=\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{z}} \pi \mathrm{r}_0^2-\mathrm{LI}$
Since, $R=0$, so $\phi=B_0(1-\alpha z) \pi r_0^2-L I=$ constant

$$

$$

The lorentz force acting on the ring (which can only be vertical, because of the symmetry of the assembly) can be expressed as
$$
\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{z}}=-\mathrm{B}_{\mathrm{r}} \mathrm{I}(\mathrm{z}) 2 \pi \mathrm{r}_0=-\frac{2 \mathrm{~B}_0^2 \alpha \beta \pi^2 \mathrm{r}_0^4 \mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{~L}}=-\mathrm{kz}
$$
Equation of motion of the ring is $\mathrm{ma}_{\mathrm{z}}=\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{z}}-\mathrm{mg}=-\mathrm{kz}-\mathrm{mg}$
Equilibrium position $\mathrm{z}_0=-\mathrm{mg} / \mathrm{k}$
$$
\omega_0=\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}
$$

The lorentz force acting on the ring (which can only be vertical, because of the symmetry of the assembly) can be expressed as
